evals.name: tulip-progressive-development
evals.prompt: 加爾文主義 TULIP 是聖經的教導嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should mention TULIP acronym is a later English mnemonic (~20th c.), five points from Dort 1618-1619, Calvin never articulated these exact 5 points, present progressive development timeline

assertions.text: Mentions TULIP acronym is a later creation, not from Dort itself
Mentions Calvin died 1564 before Dort
Presents Romans 9 as national/covenantal, not individual predestination
Does not present Dort as originating Calvinism (progressive development)

evals.name: john-10-30-observation-vs-interpretation
evals.prompt: 約翰福音 10:30 「我與父原為一」的希臘原文分析
evals.expected_output: Should state hen is neuter (grammatical fact) and present multiple scholarly interpretations (shared purpose vs shared nature) without asserting one as settled by grammar

assertions.text: States hen is neuter, not masculine heis (grammatical fact)
Presents at least 2 scholarly interpretations of hen
Does NOT gloss hen as ONLY 'unity of purpose' without noting debate
Clearly separates grammatical observation from theological interpretation

evals.name: hebrews-8-13-kainos-semantic-range
evals.prompt: 希伯來書 8:13 是說舊約被廢棄嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should present kainos semantic range from BDAG, note that grammar alone doesn't settle renewal vs replacement debate, show Jeremiah 31 context

assertions.text: Distinguishes kainos (qualitative) from neos (temporal)
Cites standard lexicon (BDAG, Thayer, or similar) for kainos definition
Does NOT assert kainos definitively means 'renewed' without noting the range
References Jeremiah 31:31-33 and Torah internalization

evals.name: psalm-46-10-harpu-semantic-range
evals.prompt: 詩篇 46:10 「你們要休息」的希伯來文原意是什麼？
evals.expected_output: Should present raphah root meaning range, note military context of Psalm 46, but not overstate as exclusively 'military command'

assertions.text: Identifies harpu from root raphah with Hifil imperative form
Notes Psalm 46 military context (wars, kingdoms, weapons)
Does NOT call it exclusively a 'military command' (presents semantic range)
Contrasts with common devotional reading while noting the range

evals.name: almah-isaiah-7-14-semantic-range
evals.prompt: 以賽亞書 7:14 的 almah 是處女的意思嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should present almah semantic range (young woman of marriageable age), contrast with betulah, note LXX parthenos translation choice, present scholarly debate without overreaching

assertions.text: Defines almah as young woman of marriageable age (not exclusively virgin)
Contrasts with betulah (more specifically 'virgin')
Mentions LXX translation to parthenos and its significance
Presents the broader context of Isaiah 7:10-17 (Ahaz situation)
Flags RCUV '童女' as translation bias vs 呂振中 '少婦'
Contains [✓ verified] markers on factual claims
Includes 驗證來源 table at end of response

evals.name: church-language-detection-trinity
evals.prompt: 我想了解三位一體的聖經根據
evals.expected_output: Should detect '三位一體' as church language (post-first-century concept), redirect via AskUserQuestion with options like '第一世紀的原始概念' / '概念發展史' / '希伯來文原意', NOT immediately launch into a Trinity defense or attack

assertions.text: Detects 三位一體 as a post-first-century concept and flags it
Calls AskUserQuestion (or text fallback) BEFORE starting interpretation
Offers redirection options (第一世紀的原始概念, 概念發展史, or similar)
Does NOT immediately present a pro-Trinity or anti-Trinity argument without asking first

evals.name: denomination-routing-charismatic
evals.prompt: 我們教會牧師說要用方言禱告才有聖靈充滿，這是聖經的教導嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should detect this as charismatic/Pentecostal denomination question, consult references/denomination-claims.md, present first-century glossolalia evidence, distinguish from modern tongues practice

assertions.text: Identifies this as a charismatic/Pentecostal teaching about tongues
Fetches 1 Corinthians 12-14 and/or Acts 2 scripture text
Distinguishes first-century glossolalia (known languages in Acts 2) from modern tongues practice
References the historical origin of tongues-as-evidence doctrine (Azusa Street 1906 or Pentecostal movement)
Presents evidence without saying 'your pastor is wrong' (guides, doesn't attack)

evals.name: interfaith-buddhism-comparison
evals.prompt: 佛教的慈悲跟聖經的愛有什麼不同？
evals.expected_output: Should analyze Hebrew hesed/ahavah and Greek agape from first-century Jewish perspective, compare with Buddhist karuna/metta conceptual framework, avoid superficial 'they're basically the same' or dismissive 'Buddhism is wrong'

assertions.text: Analyzes Hebrew hesed (covenantal loyalty) and/or ahavah, not just generic 'love'
Explains Buddhist karuna/metta as a distinct conceptual framework, not a Bible equivalent
Highlights the covenantal/relational nature of biblical love vs. the universal/non-attached nature of Buddhist compassion
Does NOT dismiss Buddhism or claim biblical love is 'superior' — presents structural differences

evals.name: ccv-unavailable-ot-fallback
evals.prompt: 請用新漢語譯本分析以賽亞書 40:31
evals.expected_output: Should attempt fetch_ccv.py for Isaiah 40, handle the unavailability gracefully (CCV OT only has Genesis-Joshua + Ruth + Jonah), fall back to other translations, and inform the user

assertions.text: Attempts to fetch CCV for Isaiah and handles the unavailability
Informs user that CCV OT coverage is partial (Isaiah not available)
Falls back to alternative translations (RCUV, 呂振中, or others)
Still provides Hebrew analysis of Isaiah 40:31 (qavah = wait/hope with tension)

evals.name: followup-did-you-know-cycle
evals.prompt: 羅馬書 3:23 的原文分析
evals.expected_output: After presenting the analysis, should end with AskUserQuestion offering 4 options including a 'Did You Know?' fun fact from random_fact.py (excluding Romans). The fact should be from a DIFFERENT book than Romans.

assertions.text: Presents Romans 3:23 analysis with Greek/Hebrew terms (hamartia, Paul's argument about Jew-Gentile equality)
Does NOT frame Romans 3:23 through 'total depravity' or 'original sin' lens
Ends with AskUserQuestion or text fallback with 4 options
Fourth option is 'Did You Know?' with a fact NOT about Romans
